meta.title: bel

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 2 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: bel
root.mentor_note: bel is a small, blunt root, and every word it builds is about conflict.

attempt.direction_prompt: A calm, peaceful mood, or an aggressive, warlike one?

context_sentences: The customer grew belligerent the moment the refund was refused, raising his voice at the counter.
Both delegations arrived in a belligerent mood, and the talks collapsed within the hour.
The general’s bellicose speeches worried allies far more than they worried his intended enemy.
A bellicose tone in the press only made the border dispute harder to settle quietly.
The museum’s east wing is devoted entirely to antebellum furniture and dress.
Historians describe the antebellum economy as one built almost entirely around a single export.

construct_options.text: Belonging to the period before a particular war
Hostile and aggressive, ready to fight
Belonging to the period immediately after a war ends

context_sentences: By the time the army arrived, the rebel had already slipped across the northern border.
History remembers her as a rebel, though at the time most of her own neighbours called her a traitor.

construct_options.text: Someone who resists or rises up against an established authority
Someone eager to start a war for its own sake
Someone loyal to, and firmly supportive of, the ruling authority
